images.caption: Figure 1:Original Distribution Drift.Visualization and KL Divergence results demonstrated that existing methods, which adjust pre-trained models to align with the target distribution for learning personalized concepts, lead to distribution drift.

Figure 2:Overview of our PureCC.(a).We first fine-tune a flow model on the custom set as representation extractor.(b).During the pure learning stage, the representation extractor remains frozen and provides the target concept representation, which is then controlled by our adaptive scaleО»в‹†\lambda^{\star}toimplicitly guidethe trainable model. The trainable model is initialized from another pre-trained flow model and providesoriginal conditional predictionusing the Base Text as input. The entire pipeline is trained on the custom set usingв„’PвЂ‹uвЂ‹rвЂ‹eвЂ‹CвЂ‹C\mathcal{L}_{PureCC}andв„’CвЂ‹C\mathcal{L}_{CC}.(c).demonstrates the process of using our designedв„’PвЂ‹uвЂ‹rвЂ‹eвЂ‹CвЂ‹C\mathcal{L}_{PureCC}to purely learn the target concept in the velocity flow space.

Figure 3:Motivation of Adaptive ScaleО»в‹†\lambda^{\star}.AsmallО»\lambdacanpreserve the original modelвЂ™s behavior and capabilitiesbut leads to adecrease in the fidelityof the target concept. Conversely, whenО»\lambdaisexcessively large, the personalized concept dominates the learning objective, causing the final distribution to drift away from the original distribution. This results in adegradation of the modelвЂ™s generative ability: the underlying prompt cannot be followed and lower CLIP-T and HPSv2.1 scores.
Figure 4:Qualitative Comparison with SOTAsincluding Tuning-based methods: DreamBooth[36], DreamBooth + EWC[39], Mix-of-Show[13], CIFC[9], and Tuning-free methods: DreamO[31]UNO[46].
Figure 5:Qualitative comparison in Multi-Concept Customizationwith Mix-of-show[13], LoRA-S[53].

Figure 6:Qualitative comparison of styleвЂ“instance customizationacross different methods, including CIFC[9], B-LoRA[11], DreamO[31]. B-LoRA is a tuning-based approach specifically designed for balancing style and content adaptation. Each case combines an instance concept with a specific style.
